title: Bernard Arnault, generous patron of the arts and lover of tax breaks
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (5/6). France's richest man is also one of Europe's leading art collectors. Through the Fondation Louis Vuitton, he combines his passion for the arts with highly lucrative tax arrangements.

title: European Commission fines Google €890 million, risking Trump's ire
In the West Bank, a Palestinian hamlet faces relentless harassment by settlers
Trump threatens Iran with escalation, including strikes on civilian and nuclear infrastructure
subhead: After two weeks of strikes on Iran that yielded no progress, US President Donald Trump raised the possibility of targeting uranium enrichment sites beneath the Kolang mountain in central Iran.

title: Fields medal awarded to four young mathematicians
subhead: Recognized as the Nobel Prize in mathematics, the Fields medal honors researchers under the age of 40 for outstanding discoveries. Of the four recipients, one winner, 35-year-old Hong Wang, is only the third woman to receive the honor since its inception in 1936.

title: In Marseille, Chez Freddy's simple, sugar-coated chichis
subhead: 'Gourmet Kiosks' (2/6). Across France, small kiosks serving local specialties are a favorite stop for holidaymakers. Chez Freddy is a renowned stop for sweet fried dough.

title: The mysterious horse-shaped geoglyph England has been maintaining for thousands of years
subhead: The Uffington White Horse is one of the best known geoglyphs in the UK. Drawn around 3,000 years ago on a hillside in Oxfordshire, its chalk outline is maintained each year by volunteers through a restoration campaign.

How social media bans for young users, now in effect in France, have spread worldwide
subhead: France has decided to ban those under 15 from major platforms such as Instagram and TikTok. The country is following a global trend that has gained momentum in recent years.

title: Zelensky forced to dismiss Oleksandr Syrsky, Ukraine's armed forces chief
subhead: Yielding to mounting pressure from demonstrators calling for General Syrsky's departure, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky reversed course and has replaced him with General Mykhailo Drapaty.

title: Despite living 'like a walking dead man,' South Africa's Thabiso Zulu continues crusade against ANC corruption
subhead: For nearly a decade, the 44-year-old man has lived like a hunted animal. He sleeps in a different place almost every night and only shares meeting locations at the last moment.

title: France's agriculture law puts government in a bind
